phases.objective: Make the participant fillout path actually deliver what the designer promises: derived variables and end-of-session quality reports must persist through the offline-first pipeline to the server; quota cells must count real per-demographic completions (today the server returns 0 for everything because RLS hides the count query) and stop silently failing open; question-level flow-control targets must be honored; cohort/comparison feedback must work for anonymous participants; ${}/{{}} interpolation must be one coherent system; and the fillout screens' broken HSL-triplet CSS tokens plus the self-dismissing feedback panel must be fixed.
phases.gate: All of: `pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check && test && build` green with new tests (flowTarget, variableInterpolation ${} support, QuotaService cache/fail-closed, offline variable roundtrip, ReportGenerator smoke); `cargo check && cargo test --manifest-path apps/server/Cargo.toml -- --include-ignored` green including new quota_counting/cohort_stats/sync-metadata-merge integration tests against the docker test stack; and a REQUIRED live-QA pass in chromium on /q/{code}: (1) complete a questionnaire with a computed variable → session_variables rows + sessions.metadata.qualityReport visible in psql, also after an offline→online cycle, (2) welcome/consent/language/form-card surfaces render correct token colors in light+dark, (3) a branch rule targeting a question jumps to its page, (4) a gender-cell quota blocks the matching cell only, (5) an anonymous participant sees real cohort numbers in a statistical-feedback item, (6) ${var} and {{var}} both render, (7) the feedback panel waits for Continue by default.

tasks.title: Persist derived variables and quality report through the offline-first pipeline
tasks.scope: Delete FilloutRuntime's second, never-populated VariableEngine and read the real engine QuestionnaireRuntime computes into; route all variable saves through OfflineResponsePersistence.saveVariable (IndexedDB-first, D2) instead of the server-only api.sessions.upsertVariable; at completion persist the final variable snapshot plus session.metadata.qualityReport into the Dexie rows FilloutSyncEngine already ships; teach the server sync endpoint to merge session metadata into an EXISTING session row (today it only inserts); and guard the two redundant unconditional server round-trips (startSession, per-response updateSessionProgress) behind navigator.onLine. Persisting computed values also closes the F059 reproducibility concern (values are stored, never recomputed).

apps/web/src/lib/fillout/runtime/FilloutRuntime.ts (:4,:31,:54 remove duplicate engine; :88-94 startSession guard; :238-241 updateProgress; :259-271 handleComplete; :324-326 getVariables)
apps/server/src/api/sessions.rs (sync_session :2847-2909: metadata merge for existing sessions)

tasks.title: Fix broken HSL-triplet CSS tokens on participant fillout screens
tasks.scope: All participant-facing fillout chrome currently feeds bare HSL triplets (e.g. `background: var(--muted)` → `background: 210 40% 96.1%`, an invalid declaration that is dropped) into scoped component CSS: the welcome instructions box, consent muted surfaces/signature input border/destructive text, language-picker pill, the loading progress bar, and the .form-card container that hosts every form question renders with no background over the canvas. Wrap every raw color token in hsl(var(--token)) and remove the never-engaging hex fallbacks. Fillout participant surfaces ONLY — the design-system-wide token sweep belongs to P8.
tasks.approach: Tokens are defined as bare triplets in lib/styles/themes/variables.css (`--muted: 210 40% 96.1%`) and only become colors via hsl() (app.css @theme inline confirms). Mechanical fix matching the already-correct pattern in the same file (+page.svelte:821-822 uses `hsl(var(--destructive) / 0.1)`): rewrite every raw color consumption `var(--background|--foreground|--muted|--muted-foreground|--card|--card-foreground|--primary|--primary-foreground|--border|--destructive)` to `hsl(var(--…))`. Delete the dead fallbacks `var(--card, #ffffff)` / `var(--card-foreground, #0f172a)` (:856-857) and `var(--primary, #2563eb)` / `var(--primary-foreground, #ffffff)` (:873-874) — the fallback branch never engages because the custom property IS defined (as a triplet). Then grep the participant tree (`lib/fillout`, `routes/(fillout)`, `lib/runtime/timing/components`) for remaining raw `var(--` color usages and fix stragglers (skip non-color tokens like --radius). Do not touch designer/app/module surfaces.
tasks.verification: `grep -rnE 'var\(--(background|foreground|muted|card|primary|border|destructive|secondary|accent)' apps/web/src/lib/fillout 'apps/web/src/routes/(fillout)'` returns only hsl(-wrapped hits; `pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check && build`. REQUIRED live QA in chromium (light AND dark): /q/{code} welcome screen instructions box has a visible muted background; consent checkboxes/signature sections tinted, signature input shows a border, required-asterisk is red; language picker active pill highlights; loading progress bar fills violet; the form-card behind a multiple-choice question has a card background and readable text over the canvas.

tasks.title: Resolve question-id flow-control targets to their containing page
tasks.scope: FlowControlManager offers question ids as skip/branch/loop targets (labelled 'Q: …'), but QuestionnaireRuntime.handleFlowControl resolves targets exclusively via pages.findIndex(page.id === target), so a question target silently no-ops into linear next-page navigation. Add a target resolver that maps a question id to the index of the page containing it, use it in all three rule branches, and warn loudly when a target resolves nowhere instead of falling through silently.
tasks.approach: New exported pure function `resolveFlowTargetPageIndex(pages: Page[], target: string): number` in flowTarget.ts: (1) `pages.findIndex(p => p.id === target)`; (2) else find the page whose `questions` array contains target OR whose `blocks` contain it — the exact containment predicate FlowControlManager.svelte:44-49 already uses to label targets (`p.questions?.includes(id) || p.blocks?.some(b => b.questions.includes(id))`); (3) else -1. Pure module (no runtime construction needed) because QuestionnaireRuntime's constructor requires canvas/WebGL and has no existing test harness. In handleFlowControl replace both inline `this.config.questionnaire.pages.findIndex(...)` calls with the helper; when a rule has a target and the helper returns -1, `console.warn('[flow] rule %s target %s matches no page or question — falling through to next page', matchingRule.id, matchingRule.target)` before the default `navigateToPage(currentPageIndex + 1)`. No designer change — its question targets become honored.
tasks.verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test with flowTarget.test.ts: page-id target, question id in page.questions, question id inside a block, unknown id → -1, page-id wins over an identically-named question. Live QA: 3-page questionnaire, branch rule condition on q1's value targeting a question on page 3 — answering the trigger value jumps to page 3; non-trigger goes to page 2.

tasks.title: Real per-cell quota counting server-side; enforce offline; stop failing open
tasks.scope: Two server defects: (a) quota_status pushes total_completed as every cell's count, ignoring quota conditions; (b) WORSE — discovered during planning: quota_status counts sessions on the bare app pool (&state.pool) with no RLS GUC, and the 00021 dual-path SELECT policy hides every row from qdesigner_app there, so total_completed is ALWAYS 0 in production and quotas are completely inert. Fix with aggregate-only SECURITY DEFINER counting functions plus per-quota condition evaluation against completed sessions' urlParams/session-variables. Client side: cache the last good quota snapshot so the offline start path can enforce quotas, and stop swallowing quota-check failures as silent allows.
tasks.approach: Migration 00027 creates two functions owned by the migration role `qdesigner` (table owner; `sessions` is not FORCE'd, so owner-definer bypasses RLS — the same posture reasoning as ADR 0013/0022): `fillout_completed_count(qid uuid) RETURNS bigint` and `fillout_quota_condition_count(qid uuid, var_name text, op text, cmp_value text) RETURNS bigint`, both `SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public, pg_temp`, `REVOKE ALL FROM PUBLIC; GRANT EXECUTE TO qdesigner_app`. The condition variant counts completed sessions where `val := COALESCE(s.metadata->'urlParams'->>var_name, (SELECT sv.variable_value #>> '{}' FROM session_variables sv WHERE sv.session_id = s.id AND sv.variable_name = var_name))` satisfies op: ==/!= as text equality, >,<,>=,<= as numeric compare guarded by a numeric-regex check so bad data counts as non-match. Aggregate-only return (a count) so nothing per-session leaks through the definer. quota_status: keep the settings load, replace the loop body — parse each enabled quota's `condition` with the SAME grammar the client uses (QuotaService.evaluateQuotaCondition :99-157: trim; 'true'/'1' catch-all → completed_count; regex ^(\w+)\s*(==|!=|>=|<=|>|<)\s*(.+)$ with quoted-string stripping) and call fillout_quota_condition_count per quota; unparseable condition → fall back to fillout_completed_count (mirrors client allow-by-default) with a tracing::warn. total_completed also switches to the definer fn (fixing the always-0 bug). Client: fetchQuotaStatus caches every successful snapshot to localStorage key `qd-quota-status:{questionnaireId}` ({fetchedAt, statuses}) and THROWS on !res.ok/network error instead of returning []; checkQuotas catches, falls back to the cached snapshot, and when neither exists returns `{allowed: true, unchecked: true, fullQuotas: []}` (add `unchecked?: boolean` to QuotaCheckResult). +page.svelte :417: drop the `navigator.onLine &&` gate so offline starts evaluate against the cached snapshot; while online retry the check once on failure; when the result is `unchecked`, record `quotaUnchecked: true` into the session metadata (online create payload :341-352 / offline createSession metadata arg :397-405) instead of silently proceeding, so researchers can filter unverified completions.
tasks.verification: cargo test quota_counting.rs (fixture pool via DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS per tests/rbac_integration.rs pattern): seed a published questionnaire with settings.quotas of two cells (gender==male target 2, gender==female target 2) + 3 completed sessions with metadata.urlParams.gender male/male/female + 1 in-progress → assert per-cell counts 2/1 and total 3; SET LOCAL ROLE to a non-owner test role and assert EXECUTE on the fn succeeds while direct `SELECT count(*) FROM sessions` sees 0 (proves the definer is the only aggregate window). pnpm test QuotaService.test.ts (mock fetch + localStorage): per-cell isFull only blocks matching condition; fetch failure → cached snapshot used; no cache → unchecked flag. Live QA: publish with a male-cell target of 1, complete one ?gender=male session; a second ?gender=male visit hits the over-quota screen while ?gender=female proceeds.

tasks.title: Anonymous-safe cohort feedback path (public aggregate stats endpoint)
tasks.scope: Cohort and participant-vs-cohort statistical-feedback modes are dead for participants: /api/sessions/aggregate|compare take the AuthenticatedUser extractor (401 before RLS) and verify_questionnaire_access, so the engine's fetch fails and the panel renders a null 'No data' point. Add a public, aggregate-only cohort-stats endpoint for published questionnaires (min-N floored), rewire the feedback engine's cohort modes onto it using the participant's own local variable value for the participant point, and explicitly gate participant-vs-participant as researcher-only with a designer warning and an honest runtime message.
files: apps/server/src/api/sessions.rs (new public_cohort_stats handler beside quota_status; reuse NumericStatsSummary/compute_numeric_stats shapes)
tasks.approach: Migration 00028 adds `fillout_cohort_stats(qid uuid, source text, key text) RETURNS TABLE(n bigint, mean float8, std_dev float8, min float8, max float8, median float8, p90 float8, p95 float8, p99 float8)` — SECURITY DEFINER owned by qdesigner (same grants/search_path posture as P1-T4's functions), computing percentile_cont/avg/stddev over COMPLETED sessions' numeric values, mirroring load_numeric_samples' source resolution (sessions.rs :2191-2237: source='variable' → session_variable_index.numeric_value with session_variables fallback; source='response' → the responses branch). Aggregate-only in SQL so per-session values never cross the definer boundary. Handler `public_cohort_stats(State, Path(id), Query{source, key})`: verify the questionnaire exists AND status='published' (mirror sync_session's gate :2853-2859) else 404; call the fn; apply a min-N floor — n < 5 returns n plus null stats (documented in the utoipa annotation) to prevent small-cohort deanonymization; respond with the SessionAggregateResponse-compatible stats shape the client already parses. Mount in questionnaire_routes next to quota-status (public, set_rls_context layer is fine since the definer fn provides visibility). Client: sessionAnalytics gains `aggregatePublic(questionnaireId, source, key)` using raw fetch against `${VITE_API_URL}/api/questionnaires/{id}/cohort-stats` (same pattern as QuotaService — avoids an OpenAPI contract regen); engine.ts 'cohort' mode calls it in runtime mode; 'participant-vs-cohort' builds the participant point from the LOCAL runtime `variables` (resolveVariableValue on dataSource.currentVariable || key — the participant's own computed score is already client-side, and anonymous callers could never read it back via /aggregate anyway) plus cohort stats from aggregatePublic, computing zScore client-side exactly as buildParticipantVsCohortSeries does (:113-121). 'participant-vs-participant' keeps sessionAnalyticsService.compare but throws a clear 'Participant comparison requires a signed-in researcher and is not shown to participants' so StatisticalFeedback.svelte's loadError (:45-62) surfaces an honest message instead of 'No data'. Designer: warning paragraph rendered when sourceMode === 'participant-vs-participant' stating it will not render for anonymous participants.
tasks.verification: cargo test cohort_stats.rs: seed published questionnaire + 6 completed sessions with a numeric session_variable → fn returns n=6 with correct mean/stddev; unpublished questionnaire → handler 404 (SQL-level assert on the fn + status gate query); n=3 → nulls. pnpm test engine.test.ts (mock fetch): cohort mode hits the public endpoint with NO Authorization header and builds points; participant-vs-cohort uses local variable + cohort stats; p-vs-p rejects with the researcher-only message. Live QA (required): anonymous chromium session at /q/{code} with a cohort-mode statistical-feedback item after 5+ seeded completions → real cohort mean renders (no 'No data'); participant-vs-cohort shows Participant vs Cohort bars.

tasks.approach: variableInterpolation.interpolateVariables gains a second replace pass over /\$\{([^}]+)\}/g routed through the identical lookup: `expression in variables` → formatValue, else evaluateExpression via FormulaParser/ASTEvaluator, else leave the original text (extract the shared substitution callback so both regexes use one implementation). hasInterpolation matches either syntax; extractVariables scans both. Because ModularRenderer.processedItem already calls this function on text/instruction/config strings (:122-134) with the live runtime variables (activePresentation.variables = QuestionnaireRuntime's engine.getAllVariables()), extending the shared function makes ${totalScore} work everywhere {{totalScore}} already does. Do NOT write into the scriptingEngine singleton — it remains designer/scripting-tooling only.
tasks.verification: pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test with variableInterpolation.test.ts: '{{score}}' and '${score}' both substitute; mixed template; expression forms '${score * 2}' and '{{score * 2}}'; unmatched '${nope}' stays literal; hasInterpolation true for each syntax. pnpm check (InstructionProps change compiles all instruction modules). phases.objective: Close the confirmed FE and BE security findings in the charter: stored XSS via unsanitized marked+{@html}, main-thread/worker script-sandbox escapes, JWT (esp. refresh) in localStorage; and backend auth brute-force/enumeration gaps (XFF-keyed rate limit, no verify-code lockout/send cap, timing oracle), unvalidated anonymous media upload, WS token in the URL, dev router shipping in release, verify_domain with no ownership proof, 23505→409 mapping, CatchPanicLayer gaps, and the RLS anonymous-session INSERT user_id gap. Also strip production console.* including the login-email PII leak.
phases.gate: All type/test/build gates green — pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check|test|build, pnpm --filter @qdesigner/scripting-engine test, cargo check + cargo test --include-ignored — AND live-QA in a real browser proving: (a) fillout renders researcher HTML with an `` payload that does NOT execute while media images still render; (b) login + silent refresh work with the refresh token no longer in localStorage (httpOnly cookie present); (c) Yjs collaboration connects/reconnects with no `?token=` in the WS URL; (d) anonymous media upload rejects oversized/wrong-MIME/magic-byte-mismatch and accepts a real image; (e) verify-code lockout after N failures and the send cap are observable regardless of XFF rotation.

tasks.title: Harden main-thread + worker script sandboxes against bracket-notation constructor escapes
tasks.scope: Broaden the static escape guard to catch computed/quoted constructor & prototype access in all three sandbox implementations, resolve the inert strict-mode guard in the worker, and document the residual main-thread limits (full worker migration is blocked by the synchronous hook contract).
tasks.approach: (1) Broaden BANNED_PROPERTY_ACCESS (ScriptExecutor.ts:11-12) and BANNED_PATTERN (ScriptWorker.ts:45, ScriptEngine.ts:217) to also match computed/quoted access, e.g. /(?:\.\s*|\[\s*['"])(constructor|prototype|__proto__|__define[GS]etter__|__lookup[GS]etter__)\b/ , which rejects the confirmed PoC `[]['constructor']['constructor']('return this')()` and `["__proto__"]`. (2) Resolve F004 honestly: `"use strict"` is illegal together with `with(sandbox)`, so the current placement inside the with-body (ScriptWorker.ts:172-173) is inert by design. Either remove it with an explanatory comment (keeping the Proxy `with` free-variable trap) OR refactor ScriptWorker/ScriptEngine to pass SAFE_GLOBALS as named `new Function` parameters plus a real `"use strict"` prologue and drop `with` (recommended — they only need the known globals). (3) Add a worker-egress hardening note recommending a `connect-src 'none'` CSP since the worker escape's stated impact is exfiltrating the passed `context`. Update ScriptExecutor.security.test.ts (the comments at :29-30/:79-81 currently DOCUMENT the gap) to assert the bracket-notation PoC now returns {} / is rejected; add an equivalent ScriptWorker sandbox test. Add a code comment + finding note that fully isolating ScriptExecutor would require moving hook execution into the worker, which is blocked by the synchronous hook API consumed at QuestionnaireRuntime.ts:748/942/995 (executeOnMount/executeOnValidate results used synchronously) — recommend as a follow-up async refactor, out of scope here.

Note residual risk in the PR body: string-concatenation obfuscation (`['con'+'structor']`) remains defeatable by static regex on the main thread.

tasks.scope: Purge participant response/variable/event data from IndexedDB after a successful final sync and via an explicit shared-device 'end session / clear local data' action, without ever dropping unsynced records.

tasks.title: Auth endpoint hardening: peer-IP rate key, verify-code lockout + send cap, dummy-Argon2, max password, 23505→409
tasks.scope: Rekey the auth rate limiter off the socket peer IP; add per-email verify-attempt lockout, send/resend cap, and a user-existence gate; run a dummy Argon2 verify on login misses to kill the timing oracle; bound password length; and map unique-violation (23505) races to 409 instead of 500.
tasks.approach: F006: in rate_limit_middleware (rate_limit.rs:81-104) replace the raw `x-forwarded-for` fallback with the socket peer IP read from `request.extensions().get::<axum::extract::ConnectInfo<SocketAddr>>()`; wire it by changing main.rs:206 to `axum::serve(listener, app.into_make_service_with_connect_info::<SocketAddr>())`. Optionally add a trusted-proxy-hops config that indexes from the right of XFF, defaulting to peer IP. F009: add two RateLimiter instances to AppState (state.rs) constructed in main.rs — a send cap (~3/15min, key `authsend:{email}`) and a verify-attempt limiter (~5/15min, key `authverify:{email}`), reusing the existing Redis+in-memory RateLimiter so the in-memory fallback still enforces when Redis is down. In send_verification_code (auth.rs:388-409): only proceed if a user row exists for the email (SELECT EXISTS) and the send cap allows. In verify_code (auth.rs:492-535): consume the verify limiter on each attempt, and on lockout invalidate the stored code (UPDATE email_verification_codes SET used_at=NOW()); shorten the TTL (10→5 min at :395) and/or widen the code. F013: in login (auth.rs:115-135), when the user row (:126) or password_hash (:131) is absent, run password::verify_password against a fixed precomputed decoy Argon2 hash (add DUMMY_HASH to password.rs) before returning Unauthorized so all paths pay the Argon2 cost. F007: add `length(min=8, max=128)` to LoginRequest.password (models.rs:50), RegisterRequest.password (:76), PasswordResetConfirm.new_password (:118). F082: add `ApiError::from_db_error(sqlx::Error)` in error.rs mapping `err.as_database_error().and_then(|e| e.code()) == Some("23505")` → Conflict else Database, and apply it to register's INSERT (auth.rs:52-63) so the SELECT-EXISTS→INSERT TOCTOU race returns 409 not 500.
tasks.verification: cargo check + cargo test --include-ignored (add unit tests: rate_limit keys by peer IP not XFF; from_db_error maps 23505→Conflict; models reject >128-char passwords). Manual/live QA: repeatedly POST /api/auth/verify-email/verify with wrong codes for one email while rotating X-Forwarded-For → lockout still trips after N; send cap trips; login response time for an unknown email is comparable to a known email; a concurrent duplicate register returns 409.

Factor a shared `validate_upload(bytes, declared_content_type) -> Result<(mime, ext)>` and use it in upload_session_media (media.rs:526-600) and upload_media (:135-179). tasks.title: Router/transport security: WS token off URL, CatchPanic on auth+ws, dev router out of release, verify_domain DNS challenge
tasks.scope: Move the WS JWT out of the URL query into the Sec-WebSocket-Protocol handshake, add CatchPanicLayer to auth_routes and ws_route, compile the dev bootstrap router out of release builds, and replace verify_domain's unconditional auto-verify with a DNS TXT ownership challenge.
tasks.approach: F008: change ws_upgrade (handler.rs:25-34) to read the token from the `Sec-WebSocket-Protocol` header instead of `Query<WsQuery>` (delete WsQuery:18-22). Adopt a subprotocol scheme (e.g. two tokens `['qde-auth', <jwt>]`), parse the jwt, verify_access_token, and echo the accepted subprotocol back on the WebSocketUpgrade so the handshake completes. Update the two FE clients to pass the token via `new WebSocket(url, [...])` and drop `?token=`: ws.ts:85-86 and YjsProvider.ts:57-119. F085: add `.layer(CatchPanicLayer::new())` to auth_routes (mod.rs:43-46, after the rate_limit layer) and to ws_route (mod.rs:250) — keep this in mod.rs, NOT the base router in main.rs, to stay disjoint from P2-T5's main.rs edits. F012: change the dev-router mount (mod.rs:270-292) to `#[cfg(debug_assertions)]` only and drop the DEV_HELPERS_ENABLED env escape; mirror in dev.rs dev_helpers_enabled() (:44-54). Replace verify_domain (organizations.rs:1495-1528) unconditional `UPDATE organization_domains SET verified_at=NOW()` with a real ownership proof: read/generate a per-domain challenge token, resolve TXT at `_qdesigner-verify.<domain>`, and set verified_at only when it matches (else 422/409). tasks.title: Move refresh token to httpOnly cookie; keep only in-memory access token
tasks.scope: Store the refresh token in an httpOnly, Secure, SameSite cookie set by the server; keep only the short-lived access token in memory on the client; stop persisting either token in localStorage. Depends on P2-T5 because both edit auth.rs and models.rs.
tasks.approach: Backend: enable the axum-extra `cookie` feature (Cargo.toml). In login (auth.rs:175-188), register (:81-93) and refresh (:201-262) return `(CookieJar, Json<AuthResponse>)` setting a refresh_token cookie `HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict; Path=/api/auth; Max-Age=<refresh exp>`; refresh reads the token from the cookie (fall back to RefreshRequest body during transition — make it Optional in models.rs:82-84), rotates it via the existing session::revoke_refresh_token/store_refresh_token logic, sets the new cookie, and drops refresh_token from the JSON body (or leaves it empty) so JS never receives it. logout clears the cookie. Frontend: set `credentials: 'include'` on authApiClient (auth.ts:13-20) and generatedClient (runtime.ts:6-16) so the cookie flows. In auth.ts stop persisting tokens: storeSession (:322-334) writes only non-secret user/expiry (or nothing), getStoredSession (:336-345)/init (:110-125) no longer restore tokens and instead bootstrap via a silent refresh call; refreshSession (:260-277) calls the endpoint with no body relying on the cookie; keep accessToken only in the in-memory `session`; clearSession already removes the localStorage key. CSRF stays covered by the global X-Requested-With gate (csrf.rs) + SameSite; CORS already sets allow_credentials(true). Note the Secure-on-http-localhost browser exception; if non-localhost http dev is needed, gate Secure behind a config flag. Regenerate OpenAPI/contract types after the AuthResponse change. FALLBACK if cross-origin cookies prove infeasible within the task: at minimum set the refresh token as an httpOnly cookie and keep the access token in memory, even if the response body still returns tokens.
tasks.verification: cargo check + cargo test --include-ignored; pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check|test|build. Live QA (mandatory): login → DevTools shows an httpOnly refresh_token cookie and NO refresh token in localStorage (qdesigner-auth); reload the app and confirm a silent refresh re-establishes the session; confirm rotation on refresh and cookie cleared on logout; confirm cross-origin dev (5173→4100) still authenticates.

phases.gate: All of the following proven, not assumed: (1) on a FRESH database (docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml) `REQUIRE_DB=1 cargo test --manifest-path apps/server/Cargo.toml -- --include-ignored` self-provisions schema and passes with ZERO "skipping" lines, and the same command exits non-zero when postgres is stopped (hard-fail demonstrated, no false green); (2) app-pool tests run as qdesigner_app (SELECT current_user asserted); (3) new HTTP-harness suites pass, including at least 6 explicit cross-tenant mutation-DENIAL assertions through api/access::* and anonymous-session dual-path isolation at the HTTP layer; (4) MinIO storage + media-proxy Range/206 + email-verification tests pass locally and in the CI backend job with minio/mailpit services; (5) the new e2e-fullstack CI job runs test:e2e:fullstack AND test:e2e:visual green against a real backend stack, and a deliberately broken snapshot turns it red; (6) `pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check && pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web test` green with the new component-test files running (file count grows from the 43/716 baseline); (7) no remaining unconditional-skip paths in CI lanes.
phases.objective: Make the test suite incapable of lying: DB-backed Rust integration tests must provision their own schema and hard-fail (not silently skip-pass) when infrastructure is missing, run as the production-posture non-BYPASSRLS role, and gain the missing coverage layers — a tower/axum HTTP harness with explicit cross-tenant mutation-denial cases for the api/access::* gate, MinIO + email-verification integration tests, fullstack + visual Playwright lanes actually executing in CI against a real backend stack, and @testing-library/svelte component tests for the god components and shared form/UI primitives.

(2) F084: in sessions/filter.rs, stop binding every value as text — introduce enum FilterParam { Text(String), BigInt(i64), Timestamp(DateTime<Utc>) }, choose the target type from the field allowlist (reaction_time_us -> i64; started_at/completed_at -> RFC3339 timestamptz; status/participant_id/metadata.*/variable.* -> text), return ApiError::Validation (422) on parse failure instead of a Postgres execution error 500, and collapse the six duplicated operator match arms (:3224-3255) into an operator->SQL-token lookup with a single param-push path. allTasks.title: Real per-cell quota counting server-side; enforce offline; stop failing open
allTasks.scope: Two server defects: (a) quota_status pushes total_completed as every cell's count, ignoring quota conditions; (b) WORSE — discovered during planning: quota_status counts sessions on the bare app pool (&state.pool) with no RLS GUC, and the 00021 dual-path SELECT policy hides every row from qdesigner_app there, so total_completed is ALWAYS 0 in production and quotas are completely inert. Fix with aggregate-only SECURITY DEFINER counting functions plus per-quota condition evaluation against completed sessions' urlParams/session-variables. Client side: cache the last good quota snapshot so the offline start path can enforce quotas, and stop swallowing quota-check failures as silent allows.
allTasks.approach: Migration 00027 creates two functions owned by the migration role `qdesigner` (table owner; `sessions` is not FORCE'd, so owner-definer bypasses RLS — the same posture reasoning as ADR 0013/0022): `fillout_completed_count(qid uuid) RETURNS bigint` and `fillout_quota_condition_count(qid uuid, var_name text, op text, cmp_value text) RETURNS bigint`, both `SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public, pg_temp`, `REVOKE ALL FROM PUBLIC; GRANT EXECUTE TO qdesigner_app`. The condition variant counts completed sessions where `val := COALESCE(s.metadata->'urlParams'->>var_name, (SELECT sv.variable_value #>> '{}' FROM session_variables sv WHERE sv.session_id = s.id AND sv.variable_name = var_name))` satisfies op: ==/!= as text equality, >,<,>=,<= as numeric compare guarded by a numeric-regex check so bad data counts as non-match. Aggregate-only return (a count) so nothing per-session leaks through the definer. quota_status: keep the settings load, replace the loop body — parse each enabled quota's `condition` with the SAME grammar the client uses (QuotaService.evaluateQuotaCondition :99-157: trim; 'true'/'1' catch-all → completed_count; regex ^(\w+)\s*(==|!=|>=|<=|>|<)\s*(.+)$ with quoted-string stripping) and call fillout_quota_condition_count per quota; unparseable condition → fall back to fillout_completed_count (mirrors client allow-by-default) with a tracing::warn. total_completed also switches to the definer fn (fixing the always-0 bug). Client: fetchQuotaStatus caches every successful snapshot to localStorage key `qd-quota-status:{questionnaireId}` ({fetchedAt, statuses}) and THROWS on !res.ok/network error instead of returning []; checkQuotas catches, falls back to the cached snapshot, and when neither exists returns `{allowed: true, unchecked: true, fullQuotas: []}` (add `unchecked?: boolean` to QuotaCheckResult). +page.svelte :417: drop the `navigator.onLine &&` gate so offline starts evaluate against the cached snapshot; while online retry the check once on failure; when the result is `unchecked`, record `quotaUnchecked: true` into the session metadata (online create payload :341-352 / offline createSession metadata arg :397-405) instead of silently proceeding, so researchers can filter unverified completions.
allTasks.verification: cargo test quota_counting.rs (fixture pool via DATABASE_URL_MIGRATIONS per tests/rbac_integration.rs pattern): seed a published questionnaire with settings.quotas of two cells (gender==male target 2, gender==female target 2) + 3 completed sessions with metadata.urlParams.gender male/male/female + 1 in-progress → assert per-cell counts 2/1 and total 3; SET LOCAL ROLE to a non-owner test role and assert EXECUTE on the fn succeeds while direct `SELECT count(*) FROM sessions` sees 0 (proves the definer is the only aggregate window). pnpm test QuotaService.test.ts (mock fetch + localStorage): per-cell isFull only blocks matching condition; fetch failure → cached snapshot used; no cache → unchecked flag. Live QA: publish with a male-cell target of 1, complete one ?gender=male session; a second ?gender=male visit hits the over-quota screen while ?gender=female proceeds.

allTasks.scope: Purge participant response/variable/event data from IndexedDB after a successful final sync and via an explicit shared-device 'end session / clear local data' action, without ever dropping unsynced records.
allTasks.approach: Add `db.purgeSyncedSessionData(sessionId)` deleting only rows with `synced === 1` across filloutSessions/filloutResponses/filloutEvents/filloutVariables for that session, and `db.clearAllFilloutData()` for the shared-device action. Call purgeSyncedSessionData from FilloutSyncEngine after the server acks the session+responses+events+variables (post-markSynced). Add a visible 'End session / clear this device' control on the completion screen in (fillout)/q/[code]/+page.svelte wired to clearAllFilloutData, with an offline/unsynced warning. COORDINATION: this is adjacent to the offline-persistence findings F055–F057 (owned by another phase) that make variables persist+sync — purge MUST run strictly after sync ack and only on synced rows so it does not undermine those fixes; land after or in coordination with that work.

allTasks.title: Auth endpoint hardening: peer-IP rate key, verify-code lockout + send cap, dummy-Argon2, max password, 23505→409
allTasks.scope: Rekey the auth rate limiter off the socket peer IP; add per-email verify-attempt lockout, send/resend cap, and a user-existence gate; run a dummy Argon2 verify on login misses to kill the timing oracle; bound password length; and map unique-violation (23505) races to 409 instead of 500.
allTasks.approach: F006: in rate_limit_middleware (rate_limit.rs:81-104) replace the raw `x-forwarded-for` fallback with the socket peer IP read from `request.extensions().get::<axum::extract::ConnectInfo<SocketAddr>>()`; wire it by changing main.rs:206 to `axum::serve(listener, app.into_make_service_with_connect_info::<SocketAddr>())`. Optionally add a trusted-proxy-hops config that indexes from the right of XFF, defaulting to peer IP. F009: add two RateLimiter instances to AppState (state.rs) constructed in main.rs — a send cap (~3/15min, key `authsend:{email}`) and a verify-attempt limiter (~5/15min, key `authverify:{email}`), reusing the existing Redis+in-memory RateLimiter so the in-memory fallback still enforces when Redis is down. In send_verification_code (auth.rs:388-409): only proceed if a user row exists for the email (SELECT EXISTS) and the send cap allows. In verify_code (auth.rs:492-535): consume the verify limiter on each attempt, and on lockout invalidate the stored code (UPDATE email_verification_codes SET used_at=NOW()); shorten the TTL (10→5 min at :395) and/or widen the code. F013: in login (auth.rs:115-135), when the user row (:126) or password_hash (:131) is absent, run password::verify_password against a fixed precomputed decoy Argon2 hash (add DUMMY_HASH to password.rs) before returning Unauthorized so all paths pay the Argon2 cost. F007: add `length(min=8, max=128)` to LoginRequest.password (models.rs:50), RegisterRequest.password (:76), PasswordResetConfirm.new_password (:118). F082: add `ApiError::from_db_error(sqlx::Error)` in error.rs mapping `err.as_database_error().and_then(|e| e.code()) == Some("23505")` → Conflict else Database, and apply it to register's INSERT (auth.rs:52-63) so the SELECT-EXISTS→INSERT TOCTOU race returns 409 not 500.
allTasks.verification: cargo check + cargo test --include-ignored (add unit tests: rate_limit keys by peer IP not XFF; from_db_error maps 23505→Conflict; models reject >128-char passwords). Manual/live QA: repeatedly POST /api/auth/verify-email/verify with wrong codes for one email while rotating X-Forwarded-For → lockout still trips after N; send cap trips; login response time for an unknown email is comparable to a known email; a concurrent duplicate register returns 409.

allTasks.title: Validate anonymous session media uploads (size, MIME allowlist, magic bytes, filename sanitize, body limit)
allTasks.scope: Enforce a size cap, a MIME allowlist verified against magic bytes, drop the client filename from the S3 key, and bound the request body on the anonymous session media upload (and the shared upload_media path).
allTasks.approach: Add `infer` (magic-byte sniffing) to Cargo.toml. Factor a shared `validate_upload(bytes, declared_content_type) -> Result<(mime, ext)>` and use it in upload_session_media (media.rs:526-600) and upload_media (:135-179). It must: reject over a max size (~25 MB) with 413/400; call `infer::get(&bytes)` and require the sniffed type be in an image/audio/video allowlist AND consistent with the declared content_type; return the canonical mime+extension. Replace the S3 key's raw filename component — build `sessions/{session_id}/media/{file_id}.{ext}` instead of `..._{filename}` (media.rs:571) so the untrusted client filename never lands in the key. Add a per-route `DefaultBodyLimit::max(...)` (or RequestBodyLimitLayer) on the `/{id}/media` route in mod.rs:221 so the multipart is bounded before `field.bytes()` buffers it.
allTasks.verification: cargo check + cargo test --include-ignored with a unit/integration test for validate_upload: a text/plain payload renamed .png (magic-byte mismatch) and an oversize file are rejected; a real PNG/MP3 passes. Manual/live QA: POST an oversized file and a fake .png to /api/sessions/{id}/media → 400/413; a real image → 201 with a filename-free key.

allTasks.title: Router/transport security: WS token off URL, CatchPanic on auth+ws, dev router out of release, verify_domain DNS challenge
allTasks.scope: Move the WS JWT out of the URL query into the Sec-WebSocket-Protocol handshake, add CatchPanicLayer to auth_routes and ws_route, compile the dev bootstrap router out of release builds, and replace verify_domain's unconditional auto-verify with a DNS TXT ownership challenge.
allTasks.approach: F008: change ws_upgrade (handler.rs:25-34) to read the token from the `Sec-WebSocket-Protocol` header instead of `Query<WsQuery>` (delete WsQuery:18-22). Adopt a subprotocol scheme (e.g. two tokens `['qde-auth', <jwt>]`), parse the jwt, verify_access_token, and echo the accepted subprotocol back on the WebSocketUpgrade so the handshake completes. Update the two FE clients to pass the token via `new WebSocket(url, [...])` and drop `?token=`: ws.ts:85-86 and YjsProvider.ts:57-119. F085: add `.layer(CatchPanicLayer::new())` to auth_routes (mod.rs:43-46, after the rate_limit layer) and to ws_route (mod.rs:250) — keep this in mod.rs, NOT the base router in main.rs, to stay disjoint from P2-T5's main.rs edits. F012: change the dev-router mount (mod.rs:270-292) to `#[cfg(debug_assertions)]` only and drop the DEV_HELPERS_ENABLED env escape; mirror in dev.rs dev_helpers_enabled() (:44-54). Replace verify_domain (organizations.rs:1495-1528) unconditional `UPDATE organization_domains SET verified_at=NOW()` with a real ownership proof: read/generate a per-domain challenge token, resolve TXT at `_qdesigner-verify.<domain>`, and set verified_at only when it matches (else 422/409). If adding a DNS resolver dependency is undesirable within this task, gate the resolver behind a documented feature and have the endpoint return 'pending' rather than auto-verify.
allTasks.verification: cargo check + cargo test --include-ignored. cargo build --release then confirm /api/dev/bootstrap-personas is absent (404). Live QA (mandatory): open the designer authenticated, confirm Yjs collaboration connects with NO `?token=` in the WS URL (Network tab) and reconnects after a drop; verify_domain returns not-verified for a domain lacking the TXT record and verified when the record is present.

allTasks.title: Constrain anonymous sessions_insert_dual so user_id cannot be forged
allTasks.scope: Add WITH CHECK constraints so the anonymous-admitting branches of sessions_insert_dual require sessions.user_id IS NULL, and the authenticated branch requires user_id = current_app_user_id(), closing the defense-in-depth gap where RLS would not stop an anonymous INSERT that set a victim's user_id.
allTasks.approach: New migration 00027 (00026 is latest) drops and recreates the sessions_insert_dual policy from 00021:111-124: keep the authenticated branch as `current_app_user_id() IS NOT NULL AND sessions.user_id = current_app_user_id()`; add `AND sessions.user_id IS NULL` to BOTH anonymous branches — the session-GUC branch (currently :116-117) and the bootstrap branch (currently :122-123). This is compatible with the live handler which writes user_id=NULL for anonymous callers (sessions.rs:409). Add an rls_enforcement.rs assertion: an anonymous INSERT with a non-null user_id is denied, a NULL-user_id anonymous INSERT is admitted.
allTasks.verification: Migrations apply on startup / via the test harness sqlx::migrate!. cargo test --include-ignored — new rls_enforcement assertion passes and existing dual-path session-bound/user-bound tests stay green. Smoke the anonymous create path (POST /api/sessions) still succeeds.

allTasks.title: Move refresh token to httpOnly cookie; keep only in-memory access token
allTasks.scope: Store the refresh token in an httpOnly, Secure, SameSite cookie set by the server; keep only the short-lived access token in memory on the client; stop persisting either token in localStorage. Depends on P2-T5 because both edit auth.rs and models.rs.
allTasks.approach: Backend: enable the axum-extra `cookie` feature (Cargo.toml). In login (auth.rs:175-188), register (:81-93) and refresh (:201-262) return `(CookieJar, Json<AuthResponse>)` setting a refresh_token cookie `HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict; Path=/api/auth; Max-Age=<refresh exp>`; refresh reads the token from the cookie (fall back to RefreshRequest body during transition — make it Optional in models.rs:82-84), rotates it via the existing session::revoke_refresh_token/store_refresh_token logic, sets the new cookie, and drops refresh_token from the JSON body (or leaves it empty) so JS never receives it. logout clears the cookie. Frontend: set `credentials: 'include'` on authApiClient (auth.ts:13-20) and generatedClient (runtime.ts:6-16) so the cookie flows. In auth.ts stop persisting tokens: storeSession (:322-334) writes only non-secret user/expiry (or nothing), getStoredSession (:336-345)/init (:110-125) no longer restore tokens and instead bootstrap via a silent refresh call; refreshSession (:260-277) calls the endpoint with no body relying on the cookie; keep accessToken only in the in-memory `session`; clearSession already removes the localStorage key. CSRF stays covered by the global X-Requested-With gate (csrf.rs) + SameSite; CORS already sets allow_credentials(true). Note the Secure-on-http-localhost browser exception; if non-localhost http dev is needed, gate Secure behind a config flag. Regenerate OpenAPI/contract types after the AuthResponse change. FALLBACK if cross-origin cookies prove infeasible within the task: at minimum set the refresh token as an httpOnly cookie and keep the access token in memory, even if the response body still returns tokens.
allTasks.verification: cargo check + cargo test --include-ignored; pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check|test|build. Live QA (mandatory): login → DevTools shows an httpOnly refresh_token cookie and NO refresh token in localStorage (qdesigner-auth); reload the app and confirm a silent refresh re-establishes the session; confirm rotation on refresh and cookie cleared on logout; confirm cross-origin dev (5173→4100) still authenticates.

allTasks.title: Shared test-DB bootstrap: self-provisioned migrations, REQUIRE_DB hard-fail, qdesigner_app DSN in CI

Three new integration test files cover (1) auth register→login→me→refresh→logout plus 401 on bad password and 403 on CSRF-missing POST, (2) the ADR-0013 authorization contract — since admin-table mutation RLS is permissive WITH CHECK(true), api/access::verify_project_write_access / verify_org_membership / verify_questionnaire_access are THE gate, and this suite asserts cross-tenant mutations are DENIED end-to-end, (3) anonymous fillout session lifecycle through middleware/fillout_rls_context (create → submit response → read own rows; a different anonymous session cannot read them). sequence.overview: 62 tasks across 8 phases sequenced into 16 ordered waves. Participant-critical data-integrity fixes (P1) and security blockers (P2) are front-loaded into waves 1–4, with P2's server-side hardening interleaved into wave 2 alongside the P1 dependents to keep width. The test-infrastructure chain P3-T1→T2→T3→T4 is the longest dependency chain in the graph; its head (P3-T1) is pulled forward into wave 2 so the serial harness/CI work (wave 6) lands before the high-churn refactor phases P5/P6 that depend on its e2e safety net. Typing (P4) runs in two waves around the P3 serial chain; structural cleanup (P5) and Svelte modernization (P6) follow with strict merge-order discipline; accessibility (P7) and perf/features (P8) close out. 12 of 16 waves are parallel-safe worktree fan-outs (max width 8); 4 are serial batches forced by par=false tasks (P2-T9, the P3 CI chain, P5-T3/T9, P7-T4/T8, P8-T10). Audit items: duplicate F085 is owned by P2-T7 (wave 3) and must be skipped by P5-T9 (wave 9); orphan findings F026/F066/F108 have no owning task and need explicit triage before closeout.

execution_batches.rationale: All zero-dependency P1 tasks. P1-T1 (derived-variable persistence) is the worst live data-loss bug and unblocks P1-T3/T4; P1-T2 unblocks P1-T4; P1-T7 unblocks P1-T5. P1-T6 and P1-T7 are independent runtime/panel fixes. Caution: P1-T1 and P1-T6 both touch runtime variable plumbing — keep interpolation edits in the text-rendering/scripting path and persistence edits in fillout services; merge P1-T1 first.

execution_batches.batch: Wave 2 — P1 dependents + server-side security starters + CI-chain head
execution_batches.rationale: P1-T3/T4 consume wave-1 output and unblock P1-T5. P2-T3/T5/T6/T8 are dependency-free security fixes on disjoint surfaces (build config, Rust auth handlers, Rust media handlers, SQL policy migration) with no P1 file overlap. P3-T1 (test-DB bootstrap) is the head of the longest dependency chain — front-loading it accelerates the CI safety net; it touches only Rust test scaffolding.

execution_batches.batch: Wave 3 — P1 close-out + client-side security bulk
execution_batches.rationale: P1-T5 (deps P1-T4+P1-T7) completes the participant-critical phase. P2-T1 (DOMPurify sinks), P2-T2 (sandbox hardening in scripting-engine), P2-T4 (IndexedDB purge — held out of wave 2 to avoid overlapping P1-T4's offline-enforcement edits), and P2-T7 (router/transport) sit on largely disjoint surfaces. Overlap watch: P1-T5 registers a new public route while P2-T7 reworks the router — P2-T7 owns router files, P1-T5 rebases its single route registration. P2-T7 also owns duplicate finding F085.

P2-T9 is par=false and high risk: it rewires auth token plumbing across frontend and backend (every authenticated flow, WS auth, CSRF posture). Depends on P2-T5 (merged wave 2). Must run alone with full live-QA of login/refresh/logout afterward.

P4-T1 (OpenAPI regen + api.ts typing) and P4-T3 (DynamicValue narrowing) are the two roots that fan out to the rest of P4 — land them first. P3-T5 (component tests) is independent and touches only test files. Three disjoint surfaces: contracts/api service, questionnaire-core types, test files.

Strict chain P3-T2→P3-T3→P3-T4, each par=false (shared CI config, shared tower harness scaffolding). Run sequentially in one batch. Landing the unconditional e2e lane (P3-T4) here gives the P5/P6 refactor waves a real regression net before the churn starts.

All four consume the wave-5 roots: P4-T2←T1, P4-T4/T5←T3, P4-T6←T1+T3. Disjoint files: SessionManagementService, runtime v1 contract (FormQuestionHost/moduleConfigAdapter), PropertiesPanel casts, fillout entry. Safe fan-out.

All seven are dependency-free and par=true, but this is the highest merge-conflict wave: P5-T6 (api.ts split) and P5-T5 (sync-service renames) rewrite import graphs many files reference. Discipline: P5-T1's deletion list must be verified disjoint from every other wave-8 task's edits; merge order P5-T4/T5/T2/T7/T8/T1 first, P5-T6 last with a rebase pass. P5-T8 (Rust) is fully disjoint from the six frontend tasks.

Both par=false. P5-T3 (chart-setup centralization) touches import sites across chart consumers just churned by wave 8; P5-T9 (sqlx macros) rewrites the freshly split sessions.rs from P5-T8. Run sequentially: P5-T3 then P5-T9. P5-T9 must skip duplicate finding F085 — already fixed by P2-T7 in wave 3.

Three dependency-free modernization sweeps that gate the rest of P6. Overlap watch: P6-T3's 13-file $app/stores list may intersect the admin/login screens P6-T2 rewrites — partition the file list up front so T3 skips anything T2 owns; P6-T1's $derived fixes should avoid files claimed by T2.

P6-T4 (Context seam, deps T1+T2+T3), P6-T5 (reaction sub-editors, deps T1), P6-T8 (headless FilloutPageController, deps T3). Disjoint surfaces: designer store wiring, ReactionTimeDesigner, fillout entry page. P6-T8 is high risk on the participant path — live fillout QA required before merge.

P6-T6 (ReactionLabWorkspace, deps T4+T5) and P6-T7 (PropertiesPanel, deps T4) are both large, high/med-risk decompositions on disjoint components. The wave-6 e2e lane plus wave-5 component tests are the regression net.

Six dependency-free a11y tasks on distinct components: fillout page host (T1), MultipleChoice/Matrix focus rings (T2), ConfirmDialog/Toast replacing alert() (T3), chart alt text (T5), Scale ARIA (T6), CommandPalette (T7). Runs after P6 so a11y edits land on the final decomposed components rather than being churned away.

Both par=false: P7-T4 wires ARIA through the shared form primitives (broad touch-surface consumed everywhere, deps P7-T3), P7-T8 adds the photosensitivity advisory over the fillout flow P7-T1 just restructured. Run sequentially.

execution_batches.batch: Wave 15 — Perf + feature fan-out (at width cap)
execution_batches.rationale: Eight dependency-free par=true tasks, exactly at the 8-task cap. Disjoint: server analytics/sync perf (T1), fillout mathjs lazy-load (T2), WebGLRenderer allocations (T3), designer panel primitives adoption (T5 — lands after P7-T4 so it adopts the ARIA-wired primitives), dashboard skeletons (T6), reorder affordance (T7), DNS TXT verification (T8), onboarding discovery (T9).

P8-T4 (analytics UI, deps P8-T1) then P8-T10 (GDPR account deletion, deps P8-T8, par=false — touches the auth/account surface end-to-end). Sequential batch; P8-T10 needs its own live QA of the erasure path.

phase_gates.gate: Live-browser QA of the fillout golden path (online and offline→sync): derived variables and quality report persist through IndexedDB and survive syncNow(); flow-control targets resolve; per-cell quotas enforced server-side and offline (no fail-open); anonymous cohort feedback renders. Full verification suite green (pnpm check/test/build, cargo test --include-ignored). Live QA is a required gate per project policy — type/test green is not sufficient.

/security-review pass on the branch; sandbox bracket-notation escape PoCs fail against P2-T2; no console.*/PII in a production build artifact; upload validation and rate-limit/lockout behavior covered by tests; full auth flow (login, refresh, logout, WS upgrade) live-QA'd after the httpOnly-cookie migration; sessions_insert_dual forgery attempt denied in rls_enforcement tests.

CI backend job self-provisions postgres+minio+mailpit and hard-fails (REQUIRE_DB) instead of skipping; tower harness auth/fillout round-trips and cross-tenant mutation-DENIAL suite green; e2e + visual lanes run unconditionally against a real stack with the smoke port fixed; new test-count baseline row recorded in docs/decisions/baseline.md.

pnpm --filter @qdesigner/web check green with zero new any/as-any on touched paths; contracts regenerated from the live /openapi.json and committed; DynamicValue=unknown compiles across all consumers; designer + fillout golden paths re-QA'd in browser since typing changes touched runtime adapters.

Behavior-neutral refactor proven: full frontend+backend suites and the P3 e2e lane green with test counts unchanged or higher; bundle diff reviewed confirming 6 dep removals and orphan deletion; cargo clippy clean after Tx-guard/sessions.rs split; no remaining imports of the old api.ts monolith or pre-rename sync-service names (grep gate).

Grep gates: zero $app/stores imports, zero legacy Svelte 4 component patterns, ThemeProvider deleted; designer live QA (reaction lab, properties panel, drag/edit flows) and fillout live QA (page controller) in a real browser; e2e + visual lanes green — these two decompositions are the riskiest UI changes in the plan.

Keyboard-only walkthrough of fillout golden path (focus moves on question change, visible rings, no trap escapes); axe scan reports no critical/serious violations on fillout + designer entry; screen-reader spot-check of aria-live announcements, chart alternatives, and radiogroup semantics; prefers-reduced-motion and photosensitivity advisory verified on a reaction task.

Fillout route loads without mathjs in the critical-path chunk (bundle analysis); reaction-task frame timing shows no per-frame allocation regressions; DNS TXT verification and GDPR deletion live-tested end-to-end; final full verification gate plus closeout baseline.md row and triage disposition for orphan findings F026/F066/F108.

sequence.parallelization_notes: Worktree fan-out is safe for the 12 waves marked can_parallelize=true: one worktree branch per task, merged back in the order listed in each batch rationale. Serial batches (waves 4, 6, 9, 14, 16) exist because their tasks are par=false — they edit broad shared surfaces (auth plumbing end-to-end, shared CI config/harness scaffolding, cross-cutting import touch-points, shared form primitives, account/auth surface) and must run one at a time in listed order, not fanned out. Known file-overlap hotspots inside parallel waves: (1) Wave 1 — P1-T1 vs P1-T6 both touch runtime variable plumbing; partition by file (persistence vs interpolation), merge P1-T1 first. (2) Wave 3 — P1-T5's new public endpoint vs P2-T7's router rework; P2-T7 owns router/main files, P1-T5 rebases its route registration. (3) Wave 8 is the merge-conflict peak: P5-T6 rewrites the services import graph — merge it last after all siblings; P5-T1's deletion list must be checked disjoint against every other wave-8 diff before fan-out; P5-T8 (Rust) is fully independent of the frontend six. (4) Wave 10 — pre-partition P6-T2's screen list against P6-T3's 13-file sweep so no file is claimed twice. (5) Wave 15 — P8-T5 adopts the primitives that P7-T4 (wave 14) just re-wired, which is why P8 trails P7. Audit coordination: duplicate F085 is fixed once by P2-T7 (wave 3); P5-T9 (wave 9) must mark it skipped/no_change_needed, not re-fix. Cross-phase deps are all intra-phase, so waves can in principle be compressed further by an orchestrator that overlaps batches, but the given ordering keeps every wave's dep set fully merged before fan-out.
risks: P2-T9 (httpOnly refresh cookie) is serial, high-risk, and breaks every authenticated flow if cookie attributes/CSRF interplay is wrong — budget dedicated live QA of login/refresh/logout/WS auth immediately after wave 4, and keep a revert path since it blocks nothing downstream.

P2-T2 sandbox hardening can break legitimate researcher formulas/scripts; capture a regression corpus of real ScriptEngine/VariableEngine usage before tightening, or the fix trades a security hole for silent questionnaire breakage.

The P3 chain (T1→T2→T3→T4) is the longest dependency chain and three of its four tasks are par=false; any slip delays the CI/e2e safety net that the P5/P6 refactor waves (8–12) rely on — do not let waves 8+ start before wave 6 lands.

Wave 8 concentrates the api.ts split, service renames, and dead-code purge in one fan-out — highest merge-conflict probability in the plan; enforce the stated merge order (P5-T6 last) and verify P5-T1's deletion list against sibling diffs before launching agents.

P6-T6 and P6-T8 are high-risk decompositions of the two largest live surfaces (reaction lab designer, participant fillout page); regressions hit participants directly, and per project memory live browser QA is mandatory — type/test green has repeatedly not meant working here.
P4-T3 (DynamicValue→unknown) fans out into P4-T4/T5/T6 in the next wave; a wrong shape decision forces triple rework — land it with a compat shim and review its API before wave 7 starts.
Orphan findings F026, F066, F108 are covered by no task — silent scope loss unless explicitly triaged (fix, fold into an existing task, or log as Known TODOs) before the P8 closeout gate.

Long refactor arc (waves 5–12) between the security fixes and the a11y/perf tail means the branch drifts for a while; keep each wave individually green against the full phase-gate command set so the branch is always shippable at wave boundaries.
